description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese lantern ensemble, featuring a prominent cylindrical paper lantern with Japanese calligraphy suspended above a wooden post lantern. The post lantern is mounted on a textured, circular wooden base that resembles a tree stump, and has a shoji-style frame with translucent panels. Flanking the base are two symmetrical stacks of round, flat wooden discs, possibly representing decorative or ritual elements. The paper lantern above is ribbed and white, with black ink characters, and is likely designed to be illuminated from within. The overall construction combines natural wood, paper, and subtle decorative details, evoking a classic aesthetic suitable for urban parks, shrines, or festival streets.
description: A traditional Japanese lantern set with a hanging paper lantern, a wooden post lantern on a stump base, and two stacks of round wooden discs, featuring calligraphy and shoji-style panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Japanese calligraphy on the paper lantern, the shoji-style post lantern, and both stacks of wooden discs symmetrically placed.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cylindrical shape of the paper lantern, the depth of the post lantern, and a single visible stack of wooden discs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the paper lantern, the back of the post lantern, and both stacks of discs, with no calligraphy visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cylindrical lantern, the side of the post lantern, and a single stack of discs, mirroring view 1.
